skill.name: backend-dev-guidelines
skill.description: Guidance for building Supabase and PostgreSQL backends with Edge Functions, RLS, validation, configuration, testing, monitoring, email, and payments.
skill.summary: Build consistent Supabase backends with TypeScript, PostgreSQL, RLS, Edge Functions, and production-ready integration patterns.
skill.author: DojoCodingLabs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, filesystem, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but manual review found they are Markdown documentation examples. No executable scripts, prompt-injection attempts, hidden exfiltration logic, or malicious intent were found. Publish as low risk because the skill teaches backend operations that involve commands, secrets, and deployments.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples, Not Runtime Execution
low_findings.description: False positive. The flagged command patterns are fenced Markdown examples for Supabase CLI, curl, tests, imports, or directory paths. They are not executable skill code and no command runner is defined.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected locations are documentation snippets inside Markdown fences. I found no wrapper, hook, or script that would execute these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Environment And Secret References Are Instructional
low_findings.description: False positive with minor operational sensitivity. The files discuss environment variables and placeholder secrets while also warning users not to hardcode or commit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is secret-management guidance, not credential harvesting. Placeholder secret values appear in examples and are paired with instructions for environment storage.

low_findings.title: Filesystem And Path Traversal Matches Are Examples
low_findings.description: False positive. The filesystem matches are import paths, local configuration examples, and relative source references used to teach project structure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples show local project files and imports. I did not find user-controlled path concatenation in executable code.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are Documented API Examples
low_findings.description: False positive. The network findings are sample fetch, localhost curl, allowed-origin, Sentry DSN, Supabase, Stripe, and Resend integration examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network references align with the stated backend-integration purpose. No code sends local secrets to an unauthorized external endpoint.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Function Constructor Flag Is A Text Match
low_findings.description: False positive. The scripts finding points at architecture prose saying Edge Function, not JavaScript Function constructor usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected line is an ASCII architecture diagram. It contains no dynamic code execution primitive.

content.user_title: Build Supabase Backends With Clear Guidelines
content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent patterns for Supabase, PostgreSQL, RLS, and Edge Functions. This skill gives practical guidance for secure APIs, integrations, testing, and deployment.
actual_capabilities: Explains Supabase Edge Function structure, routing, validation, and response patterns.
Provides PostgreSQL schema and Row-Level Security policy examples for user-owned data.
Covers configuration management, environment variables, and secret handling patterns.
Documents Resend email, Stripe payment, and webhook integration approaches.
Includes testing, monitoring, Sentry, middleware, repository, and service-layer guidance.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not an automated code generator or deployment tool.
Examples are opinionated for Supabase and may need adaptation for other backend stacks.
It does not verify that generated RLS policies are correct for every business rule.
Users must review commands, secrets, and deployment steps before running them.

use_cases.title: Design a Supabase Feature
use_cases.description: Plan tables, RLS policies, Edge Functions, validation, and tests for a new backend feature.

use_cases.title: Review Backend Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether an API separates routing, controllers, services, repositories, validation, and error handling clearly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Integrations
use_cases.description: Use consistent patterns for Stripe webhooks, Resend emails, Sentry monitoring, and secret configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Edge Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-dev-guidelines skill to outline a Supabase Edge Function for creating a post. Include validation, auth, response format, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Add RLS to a Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-dev-guidelines skill to design PostgreSQL tables and RLS policies for user-owned comments. Explain each policy in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor API Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-dev-guidelines skill to refactor this route into controller, service, repository, validation, and error handling responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-dev-guidelines skill to plan a Stripe webhook Edge Function with secrets, signature verification, idempotency, monitoring, and local tests.

output_examples.input: Design a backend endpoint for creating posts.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering the Edge Function, Zod validation, JWT verification, RLS-backed database writes, responses, and test cases.

output_examples.input: Review my Supabase schema for access control gaps.
output_examples.output: A review checklist that highlights table ownership, RLS policy coverage, indexes, migration order, and missing authorization tests.

output_examples.input: Prepare Stripe webhook handling for production.
output_examples.output: A deployment plan covering secret setup, signature checks, idempotent event storage, Sentry monitoring, local replay testing, and rollback notes.

best_practices: Treat RLS as the primary data boundary and test each policy with realistic users.
Keep Edge Functions focused and move business logic into services or repositories.
Validate inputs, verify JWTs, and review secret-handling commands before deployment.
anti_patterns: Putting database access, validation, and business logic directly inside route declarations.
Using service-role keys or secret values in client-side code or committed files.
Deploying Edge Functions before local tests, webhook verification, and monitoring are ready.

faq.question: Does this skill run backend commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and command examples. Users must decide when to run any command.

faq.question: Which stack is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is mainly designed for Supabase Edge Functions, PostgreSQL, RLS, TypeScript, Resend, Stripe, and Sentry.

faq.question: Can it help with non-Supabase backends?
faq.answer: Yes, some routing, service, validation, testing, and configuration patterns are reusable with adaptation.

faq.question: Does it generate secure RLS policies automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives patterns and examples. Each policy still needs review against the product access model.

faq.question: Does it include deployment guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Supabase CLI examples for local serving, deployment, and secret configuration.

faq.question: What should users review most carefully?
faq.answer: Users should review secrets, service-role usage, deployment commands, webhook verification, and RLS policies.
